verify.feedback: All Tier-1 gates re-ran green live on the rebased tip (integration tip 8c8fed6; rebase was a no-op) and all declared evidence files exist and are non-empty with correct names. FAIL for one reason: review.json records approved=false with a HIGH finding that is confirmed real and unfixed — eng §4 (docs/planning/03-eng.md:172) requires `message.assigned_run_id bigint FK NULL` but db/migrations/001_core.sql:37 declares `assigned_run_id bigint` with no REFERENCES run(id). The ticket's agent-review verification item ('schema matches eng §4') is therefore unsatisfied. Fix: add `REFERENCES run(id)` to message.assigned_run_id in the migration, re-run gates, and obtain an approved review. Minor note: RBG red logs are genuine failure transcripts, but no failing-first commit exists to re-run (red captured pre-commit); acceptable content-wise, flagged for process hygiene.

findings.title: Concurrent manual triggers for the same window still send duplicate summaries
findings.detail: The D2 run claim is not used as an execution gate. `claimRun()` returns `{ claimed: false }` on the losing side of an `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, but `triggerSummary()` discards that flag and always invokes the executor. Because `executeRun()` only short-circuits after `status='posted'`, two concurrent `POST /api/trigger` calls for the same chat/window produce one `run` row and two `telegram_outbox` rows. Repro on this tree: ingest one message, then `await Promise.all([triggerPost(req()), triggerPost(req())])` for the same payload; the database ends with `outboxCount: 2` and one `run` row. That violates the ticket's D2 claim contract and means the manual run lifecycle is not actually serialized by durable Postgres state.

review.reasons: Not approved. The earlier assigned_run_id foreign-key issue is fixed and the named gates are green, but the current trigger path still allows the loser of the `(chat_id, window_start, window_end)` claim race to execute the pipeline and duplicate delivery. For this ticket, the manual trigger must treat the Postgres claim as the execution gate.
